format_metadata.subject: Forming a study group for the BIO 220 midterm

items.text:
Subject: Forming a study group for the BIO 220 midterm

Hi everyone,

I'm Priya from Tuesday's discussion section, and I'd like to start a study group for the BIO 220 midterm on Friday, October 17. A few of us have already agreed to meet, and we'd love to have more people join.

We've reserved Study Room 3B in Caldwell Library for three Sunday afternoons: October 5, 12, and 16, from 2:00 PM to 5:00 PM. The room holds eight students, so spots are limited. The library charges a refundable $5 key deposit for the room, which I'll collect at the first meeting and return once we hand the key back. Please bring your lecture notes and the practice problem set, since Professor Hale said those problems are the best guide to the exam.

If you'd like a seat, reply to me by Wednesday, October 1, so I can finalize the list. Anyone who joins is expected to prepare one chapter to explain to the group.

Thanks,
Priya

questions.stem: According to the email, what does the library charge for the use of Study Room 3B?
questions.explanation: Priya writes that the library charges a refundable $5 key deposit for the room, which she will collect and return once the key is handed back. The distractors reuse plausible campus charges (membership, supplies, late penalty) that the email never mentions.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about students who want to focus their exam preparation effectively?

options.A: They should work through the practice problem set
options.B: They should reserve their own library room
options.C: They should skip the Sunday meetings
options.D: They should pay an additional course fee

questions.explanation: Priya notes that Professor Hale said the practice problems are the best guide to the exam, implying that working through that set is the most effective way to prepare. The other options reverse or fabricate advice not supported by the email.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this email?

options.A: To report the results of the BIO 220 midterm
options.B: To recruit classmates for a midterm study group
options.C: To request a refund of a library deposit
options.D: To complain about the limited room capacity

questions.explanation: The email invites classmates to join a study group, gives meeting times and a reply deadline, and explains what to bring. Its overall purpose is to recruit members, not to report scores, request a refund, or complain about capacity.

items.text:
Subject: You're invited: Spring Robotics Showcase

Dear members,

On behalf of the Robotics Club, I'm excited to invite you to our Spring Showcase on Saturday, April 18, from 11:00 AM to 3:00 PM in the Latham Engineering Atrium. Teams will demonstrate the projects they've built this term, and visitors can vote for their favorite design.

Admission is free for current club members, but please register at the club portal by April 10 so we can print enough name badges and order food. Guests from outside the club are welcome too, though they'll be asked to pay a $3 entry fee at the door, which goes toward next year's parts budget. Light refreshments will be served, and a short awards ceremony begins at 2:30 PM.

If you'd like to help run a demonstration table, email me by April 8 and I'll add you to the volunteer schedule. We hope to see you there.

Best regards,
Theo Marsh

questions.stem: According to the email, what will visitors be able to do at the showcase?

options.A: Enroll in a new robotics course
options.D: Receive a refund of the entry fee

questions.explanation: The email states that visitors can vote for their favorite design after teams demonstrate their projects. The distractors reuse showcase-related terms (course, parts budget, entry fee) but describe actions the email never offers.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about a person who attends the showcase without belonging to the club?

options.A: They must demonstrate a project of their own
options.B: They will receive a printed name badge
options.C: They will be charged at the door
options.D: They cannot enter the engineering atrium

questions.explanation: Admission is free for current members, while outside guests are asked to pay a $3 entry fee at the door, so a non-member would be charged to enter. The other options reverse the admission rule or invent requirements not stated.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this email?

options.A: To announce the cancellation of a club event
options.B: To request volunteers for a parts budget review
options.C: To invite members to attend a club showcase
options.D: To explain how to join the Robotics Club

questions.explanation: The email invites members to the Spring Showcase, giving the date, time, location, registration deadline, and program details. Its overall purpose is to invite people to attend, not to cancel an event, review a budget, or explain club membership.
